title: Cloud Storage and S3 Management
description: Uploading, downloading, copying, and deleting objects in S3-backed storage

use_cases.name: Upload File
sub_flows: Stream upload: CreateMultipartUploadCommand → UploadPartCommand (×N, max 4 concurrent) → CompleteMultipartUploadCommand
use_cases.retry: simple_retry (3 attempts, 200ms delay) on each part

use_cases.name: Download / Read File
sub_flows: GetObjectCommand with optional Range header and VersionId

use_cases.name: Copy File
sub_flows: size ≥ 4 GiB: #copy_multipart → CreateMultipartUploadCommand → UploadPartCopyCommand (×N, 4 GiB parts) → CompleteMultipartUploadCommand
use_cases.note: src_node.get('s3:location') resolves bucket/key; progress_tracker updated after copy

use_cases.name: Create Directory
use_cases.note: Metadata-only operation; no S3 object created

use_cases.name: Stat / Read Metadata
sub_flows: Applies pending Insert/Update/Delete ops from queue
Falls back to DB read via NodeUIDSelector if diff exists

use_cases.name: List Directory
sub_flows: Recursive CTE SQL query on fsentries table

use_cases.name: Move / Rename File
use_cases.note: Metadata update only; S3 key unchanged (copy+delete pattern if needed at upper layer)

use_cases.name: Create Signed Upload URL (fsv2)
sub_flows: multipart mode: CreateMultipartUploadCommand → createSignedMultipartPartUrls (UploadPartCommand presigned URLs per part)

use_cases.name: Server-side Upload (fsv2)
sub_flows: Empty file edge case: AbortMultipart + PutObject(empty body)

key_components.name: FilesystemAPIService
key_components.role: HTTP route registrar; mounts all filesystem API routers on web-server app

key_components.name: S3StorageController
key_components.role: Primary S3 I/O controller (server-side streaming, copy, delete, read)

key_components.name: S3StorageProvider
key_components.role: Signed URL generation + server-side upload for fsv2 architecture

key_components.name: FSEntryController
key_components.role: DB metadata manager for filesystem entries (queue-based write serialization)

key_relationships: S3StorageController delegates_to FSEntryController for node metadata (s3:location, size)
FSEntryController wraps Insert/Update/Delete ops in serialized queue
FSEntryController reads/writes fsentries table via db
S3StorageController.copy reads node.get('s3:location') → bucket, key, region
important_notes: Two parallel S3 backends: legacy S3StorageController (streaming, server proxied) and fsv2 S3StorageProvider (signed URLs, client direct upload)
Multipart threshold: legacy controller uses stream-based chunking (5 MB parts); copy switches to multipart at 4 GB file size
FSEntryController uses deferred/current queue states to batch DB writes and avoid race conditions
FilesystemAPIService is event-driven: registers routes via __on_install.routes lifecycle hook
Retry logic: simple_retry(fn, 3, 200) wraps S3 commands
